# Transcrição: Links_Are_Down_on_Facebook,_But_Does_It_Matter_

## [00:00 - 01:32]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about how links are way down on Facebook. Let's go. Only 7.7% of views on Facebook were posts with links. What does that mean? 92.3% of all views in the first quarter didn't include a link. Post with links made up 13.5% of views in the third quarter of 2021. So the share of views with links has been nearly cut in half. Does that mean that the algorithm deprioritizes links or are users simply creating fewer posts with links? When you think about it, this really isn't all that difficult to understand. Reels and video have taken up a much greater share of the news feed since 2021. We don't know for sure, but the rise of videos alone could explain the drop in link percentage. As a marketer, don't feel like this means you can never share a link again. You still need to drive traffic to your blog post or opt-in or product. The video format does complicate measurement, but it's absolutely worth it. Take advantage of videos if you haven't already, but links on Facebook aren't doomed either. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate, and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
